# Auth Requirements Checklist: Google Login and Account Linking

- **Purpose**: Validate authentication, account-linking, and legacy-flow requirements quality before implementation planning
- **Created**: 2026-05-31
- **Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are Google sign-in eligibility requirements complete for buyer, seller, and admin account audiences? [Completeness, Spec section FR-001, Spec section FR-017]
- [ ] CHK002 Are requirements defined for both first-time Google sign-in and returning linked Google sign-in? [Completeness, Spec section User Story 1, Spec section User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements complete for matching a Google email to an existing password account before linking? [Completeness, Spec section FR-004]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements defined for the full linking decision path: prompt, consent, password confirmation, success, decline, and failure? [Completeness, Spec section User Story 2, Spec section FR-005-FR-009]
- [ ] CHK005 Are requirements complete for creating new Google-authenticated users as normal user accounts regardless of buyer or seller entry point? [Completeness, Spec section FR-018, Spec section FR-019]
- [ ] CHK006 Are requirements documented for preserving existing profile creation and role propagation behavior after Google-created accounts? [Completeness, Spec section FR-016, Spec section SC-005]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "verified Google email" clearly defined enough to distinguish usable, unusable, and unverified Google email states? [Clarity, Spec section FR-003, Spec section Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "normal user account" clear enough to distinguish it from seller-capable and admin accounts? [Clarity, Spec section FR-018, Spec section Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "same successful-session behavior" specific enough to support planning without reintroducing browser-readable tokens? [Clarity, Spec section FR-002, Spec section FR-012]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "no compatibility required during development" clear enough to define acceptable old URL outcomes without conflicting with required protocol behavior? [Clarity, Spec section FR-014, Spec section Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK011 Are user-facing error outcome requirements specific enough for Google cancellation, Google failure, mismatch, declined linking, incorrect password, and blocked account states? [Clarity, Spec section FR-015]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Are buyer/seller-only requirements consistent across user stories, functional requirements, success criteria, and assumptions? [Consistency, Spec section Clarifications, Spec section FR-001, Spec section SC-006]
- [ ] CHK013 Are admin exclusion requirements consistent with the legacy-flow removal story and admin sign-in acceptance criteria? [Consistency, Spec section User Story 4, Spec section FR-017, Spec section SC-007]
- [ ] CHK014 Are seller onboarding requirements consistent with the account creation requirement for new Google users from the seller app? [Consistency, Spec section FR-018, Spec section FR-019]
- [ ] CHK015 Are email-verification requirements consistent between new Google account creation and linking to an existing password account? [Consistency, Spec section FR-003, Spec section FR-020]
- [ ] CHK016 Are account-status, lockout, and role requirements consistent across password login, first-time Google login, and linked Google login? [Consistency, Spec section FR-011, Spec section User Story 3]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are primary scenarios documented for new Google users, existing password users, already-linked users, and legacy URL access? [Coverage, Spec section User Scenarios & Testing]
- [ ] CHK018 Are alternate scenarios documented for declined account linking and Google sign-in from the seller app before seller onboarding? [Coverage, Spec section User Story 2, Spec section FR-019]
- [ ] CHK018A Are requirements explicit that declined, failed, abandoned, or expired same-email linking must not create a duplicate Google-only account? [Coverage, Spec section FR-023, Spec section SC-009]
- [ ] CHK019 Are exception scenarios documented for unusable Google email, unverified Google email, blocked account status, wrong password, and abandoned linking? [Coverage, Spec section Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK020 Are recovery expectations defined for abandoned linking, expired Google sessions, and repeated wrong password attempts? [Coverage, Spec section Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK021 Are requirements defined for future sign-ins after a successful Google link so the prompt is not repeated? [Coverage, Spec section User Story 3, Spec section FR-010]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK022 Are requirements defined for Google email changes after linking and whether the linked provider identity or email match remains authoritative? [Edge Case, Spec section Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK023 Are requirements defined for existing accounts with no usable password when Google email matches the account? [Edge Case, Spec section Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK024 Are requirements defined for repeated wrong passwords during linking and alignment with existing lockout protections? [Edge Case, Spec section Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK025 Are requirements defined for old user-facing IdentityServer account URLs without requiring graceful redirect compatibility? [Edge Case, Spec section FR-014]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK026 Are success criteria measurable for link protection, email verification, and legacy-page removal? [Measurability, Spec section SC-002, Spec section SC-004, Spec section SC-008]
- [ ] CHK027 Are success criteria tied to buyer and seller app verification without implying admin Google support? [Measurability, Spec section SC-006, Spec section SC-007]
- [ ] CHK028 Is the 30-second linked Google sign-in outcome measurable from a user perspective without prescribing implementation details? [Measurability, Spec section SC-001]
- [ ] CHK029 Are zero-tolerance criteria for unsafe links objectively stated for declined consent, wrong password, abandoned linking, blocked status, and unusable Google email? [Measurability, Spec section SC-003]

## Security & Privacy Requirements

- [ ] CHK030 Are requirements explicit that matching email alone is insufficient to link an existing password account? [Security, Spec section FR-005-FR-007]
- [ ] CHK031 Are requirements explicit that verified Google email may mark account email verified only after successful password-confirmed linking? [Security, Spec section FR-020, Spec section Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK032 Are requirements defined to prevent Google sign-in from exposing browser-readable access or refresh tokens? [Security, Spec section FR-012]
- [ ] CHK033 Are account takeover prevention requirements complete for consent, password confirmation, lockout, blocked accounts, and unverified Google email? [Security, Spec section FR-005-FR-011, Spec section Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK034 Are security requirements sufficient to ensure admin accounts cannot be created or accessed through Google sign-in? [Security, Spec section FR-017]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK035 Are assumptions documented for Google providing verified email state and identity information needed for linking? [Dependency, Spec section Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK036 Are dependencies on existing profile creation, seller onboarding, role propagation, and account status rules documented? [Dependency, Spec section FR-016, Spec section FR-019, Spec section Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK037 Are assumptions about development-stage legacy URL compatibility documented and aligned with the cleanup requirement? [Assumption, Spec section FR-014, Spec section Assumptions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK038 Are all clarification decisions reflected in functional requirements and not only in the Clarifications section? [Traceability, Spec section Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK039 Are there any remaining terms such as "activate", "usable email", or "safe fallback" that need clearer acceptance criteria before planning? [Ambiguity, Spec section User Story 1, Spec section Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK040 Are old IdentityServer page removal requirements scoped clearly enough to avoid removing non-page protocol behavior still required by authentication? [Conflict, Spec section FR-013, Spec section Assumptions]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
